title: Beyond Logical Forms: LLM-Extracted Patterns for Fallacy Classification
abstract: arXiv:2606.26698v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: In today's fast-paced information era, logical fallacies, defined as defective patterns of reasoning, inevitably contribute to the growth of information disorder. However, often fallacies appear in nuanced forms that complicate automated classification. In this study, we investigate whether merging abstract logical structures with context-level linguistic cues proves beneficial for fallacy classification, developing a framework that inductively extracts such patterns from fallacious examples and their explanations using Large Language Models (LLMs). We evaluate the impact of these patterns across different LLMs and experimental zero- and one-shot configurations, showing statistically significant improvements over zero-shot baselines and outperforming competing approaches. Cross-dataset experiments validate generalization, establishing data-driven pattern extraction as an effective method for generating logical representations.

Title: Beyond Logical Forms: LLM-Extracted Patterns for Fallacy Classification
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26698v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: In today's fast-paced information era, logical fallacies, defined as defective patterns of reasoning, inevitably contribute to the growth of information disorder. However, often fallacies appear in nuanced forms that complicate automated classification. In this study, we investigate whether merging abstract logical structures with context-level linguistic cues proves beneficial for fallacy classification, developing a framework that inductively extracts such patterns from fallacious examples and their explanations using Large Language Models (LLMs). We evaluate the impact of these patterns across different LLMs and experimental zero- and one-shot configurations, showing statistically significant improvements over zero-shot baselines and outperforming competing approaches. Cross-dataset experiments validate generalization, establishing data-driven pattern extraction as an effective method for generating logical representations.
translated_title: 超越逻辑形式：基于大语言模型提取的谬误分类模式
translated_abstract: 在当今快节奏的信息时代，逻辑谬误——被定义为有缺陷的推理模式——不可避免地加剧了信息混乱。然而，谬误往往以微妙的形式出现，使得自动分类变得复杂。在本研究中，我们探讨了将抽象逻辑结构与上下文层面的语言线索相结合是否有助于谬误分类，并开发了一个框架，该框架利用大语言模型从谬误示例及其解释中归纳性地提取此类模式。我们在不同的大语言模型以及零样本和一样本实验配置中评估了这些模式的影响，结果显示，与零样本基线相比，该方法取得了统计上显著的改进，并优于其他竞争方法。跨数据集实验验证了其泛化能力，确立了数据驱动的模式提取作为生成逻辑表示的有效方法。
tldr: 本研究提出了一种利用大语言模型从谬误示例中归纳提取抽象逻辑结构与上下文语言线索相结合的模式，显著提升了谬误分类的准确性和泛化能力。
en_tdlr: This study proposes a framework that inductively extracts patterns combining abstract logical structures with contextual linguistic cues from fallacious examples using LLMs, significantly improving fallacy classification accuracy and generalization.
